title: The Energy Risk Remains (Part I)
gist: US energy stocks were the best-performing GICS sector of the past five years and delivered returns largely uncorrelated to other assets, yet they now sit at just ~3% of the S&P 500 (near record lows) while tech dominates at ~40% weight, leaving the sector deeply under-owned.

insights.claim: US energy stocks, as a GICS sector, delivered the best total return of any US sector over the past five years, and that performance was broadly uncorrelated with almost all other asset sectors and asset classes.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: An uncorrelated top-returning sector is a portfolio diversifier that few investors currently hold, making it a candidate for allocation.
insights.quote: Energy stocks, as a GICS sector, have delivered the best total return performance of any US sector over the past five years.
insights.locator: Energy stocks, as a GICS sector

insights.claim: Energy stocks rallied precisely when stocks and bonds fell after Russia's invasion of Ukraine, and gapped higher again when the loss of shipping insurance effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Energy acts as a geopolitical and inflation hedge that pays off in the exact scenarios where a stock/bond portfolio suffers.
insights.quote: Energy stocks gapped higher again when the loss of shipping insurance effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.
insights.locator: When stocks and bonds fared poorly

insights.claim: A portfolio held 50% in US tech stocks and 50% in US energy stocks (with a sprinkle of gold and precious-metal miners) over the past five years would have delivered powerful and very steady returns.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Pairing tech with energy plus gold offers a barbell that smooths returns because the legs perform in different regimes.
insights.quote: the portfolio to have had over the past five years would have been 50% invested in US tech stocks and 50% invested in US energy stocks

insights.claim: Tech stocks now account for almost 40% of the S&P 500's market capitalization, while energy stocks sit at just a 3% weight, close to record lows.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: A record-low sector weight against a record-high tech weight sets up an asymmetric contrarian/mean-reversion opportunity in energy.
insights.quote: while tech stocks now account for almost 40% of the S&P 500’s market capitalization, energy stocks, with their 3% weight, are close to record lows
insights.locator: In fact, while tech stocks now account

insights.claim: Despite energy's strong and uncorrelated performance, energy names are not popular among investors today, signaling a disconnect between fundamentals and positioning.
insights.speaker: Louis-Vincent Gave
insights.so_what: Broad investor apathy toward a proven-performing sector is a classic contrarian entry signal.
insights.quote: one might think that energy names would be all the rage among investors today. But one would be wrong.
